task_completion.description: The agent identifies or selects the correct segmentation node in the Data or Segment Editor module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'TumorSegmentation' is visible in the module panel, subject hierarchy, or node selection dropdown.

task_completion.description: The agent enables 3D visibility for the segmentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Segment Editor module, the 'Show 3D' button is depressed/active, or the 'eye' icon next to the 3D row is open/active.

task_completion.description: The 3D viewport renders the tumor structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view panel (typically the top-right or bottom-right quadrant) shows a colored 3D object (the tumor) against the background, distinct from the 2D slice views.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the process to save a screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Scene Screenshot' dialog box is open, or the 'Screen Capture' module is active with capture controls visible.

task_completion.description: The agent specifies the correct filename for the screenshot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'tumor_3d_visualization.png' is visible in the filename field of the save dialog or the Screen Capture settings.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI rather than terminal commands to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Slicer menus, buttons, and panels, not a terminal window executing scripts to generate the image.

integrity.description: The 3D view is a live rendering within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D view is contained within the Slicer application window layout, not a static image opened in an external viewer.

integrity.description: The agent does not import pre-made screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: No file browser windows showing downloads of 'tumor_3d_visualization.png' from external sources.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The segmentation node to be visualized is named 'TumorSegmentation'. The required output filename is 'tumor_3d_visualization.png'. The application being used is 3D Slicer.
